title: Friday Sermon 11 October 2002
description:
In continuation of last week’s sermon verses 11 and 12 of Surah al-Talaq [The Divorce] illustrate the Divine attribute of Al Noor or The Light - The verses depict disbelief as darkness and the knowledge of Allah’s Signs as Noor or Light:*

The abundance with which the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) recited 'The Holy Quran', in turn illuminated the world with the message of Allah.

(Abu Althana Shihabuddin Mahmud al-Alusi.  'Ruh al-Ma'ani fi Tafsir al-Quran al-Azim wa al-Sabu al-Mathānī').

…light comes from God Almighty to remedy the Age of Darkness, and that light is His Messenger and His Book.  With this light, God shows the way to those who seek His pleasure, and so God delivers them out of every kind of darkness into light, and guides them to the right path.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Barahin e Ahmadiyya: iv'.  'Ruhani Khazain'.  648, 649.  English: Islam International Publications Ltd, 2016.  61).

It is through the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings on him) that we have obtained a portion of the blessings of the treasure that was granted to him by Allah.  The beam of guidance that the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) brought spreads like sunshine:

I always wonder what a Sublime Prophet this Arabian Prophet is, whose name is MUHAMMAD (thousands upon thousands of blessings and peace be upon him).  One cannot fathom the limit of his sublime status and it is not given to man to estimate the extent of his spiritual effectiveness … He has been given the treasure house of every understanding.  He who does not receive through him is deprived forever.  I am nothing and possess nothing.  What am I and what do I amount to? I would be ungrateful if I were not to confess that I have found true Tauhid through this very Prophet.  I have attained the recognition of the Living God through this same perfect Prophet and through his light.  The honour of converse and communion with God, through which I behold His countenance, has been bestowed upon me through the same Great Prophet.  The rays from this Sun of Guidance fall upon me like sunshine and I continue to be illumined only so long as I am facing towards it.

(Ahmad.  'Ḥaqīqatul Waḥī' ['The Philosophy of Divine Revelation'].  Magazine Press, 1907.  'Rūhānī Khazāin: xxii'. 118, 119.  English: Chaudhary Muhammad Ali.  Islam International Publications Ltd, 2018.  137, 138).

* Fakhruddin al-Razi.  'Tafsir e Kabir: Mafatih al-Ghayb'.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20021011-UR.pdf
